title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring · Inference Brief: Refactor Compiler Evidence
summary: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts refactor compiler evidence increment in a behavior-and-type regression suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use rename, references, deprecation, declaration diffs, and consumer builds to bound a refactor.
Build changed-schema, invalid-value, async, and clean-consumer fixtures around public boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.

steps.title: Predict Refactor Compiler Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"4" that must not enter the trusted model.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Refactor Compiler Evidence contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use rename, references, deprecation, declaration diffs, and consumer builds to bound a refactor.

steps.title: Bound Refactor Compiler Evidence

content.text: Use rename, references, deprecation, declaration diffs, and consumer builds to bound a refactor. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Separate compile-time claims from runtime facts, narrow every external unknown with executable evidence, inspect diagnostics and emitted JavaScript, then test a changed schema without assertions or suppression. Apply that move specifically to Refactor Compiler Evidence while producing a behavior-and-type regression suite.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"4" that must not enter the trusted model. Competency probe: Use rename, references, deprecation, declaration diffs, and consumer builds to bound a refactor. A defensible solution applies Refactor Compiler Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a behavior-and-type regression suite.

content.caption: Runnable type-and-runtime contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: No local diagnostics prove a public refactor is safe. This fails the Refactor Compiler Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use rename, references, deprecation, declaration diffs, and consumer builds to bound a refactor.
